_notes.purpose: Common defaults for derived_domestic profiles. Carries the 4-component skeleton in declared order [base_sofr, demand_volatility, payment_cycle, wc] with stress_timing 12/3/9 inherited from V1, 24-month quarterly tenor grid, and 250bps default loan spread. NO V1 precedent — Iter-6 introduces derived_domestic. Never used alone; sector-specific profiles (e.g. us-services.json) override these defaults via deep merge per design-v1-config-architecture.md §4.
_notes.merge_position: Least specific. Loaded first by profile_resolver (Iter-6 deliverable 7 adds the domestic-aware branch); sector files override on top.
_notes.dispatch_default: v2_direct because no DRAPS path exists for derived_domestic. V2 computes the SOFR path directly via COMPONENT_REGISTRY per the composer's generalized derived branch (Iter-6 deliverable 4).
_notes.component_choices: [base_sofr, demand_volatility, payment_cycle, wc] matches design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §Component-to-audience-matrix 'Domestic services' column: payment_cycle_stress_dso is core; demand_volatility / wc / base_sofr universal. Iter-7 (ecommerce) substitutes inventory_carrying for payment_cycle per the same matrix; ecommerce will need its own _base or this file will be refactored.
_notes.applies_to_mode_note: applies_to.mode is the coarse classifier 'domestic' (schema enum). The state-side business_identity.mode (e.g. 'domestic_services') is the fine classifier; profile_resolver bridges coarse-to-fine via applies_to.industry. No naics field exists on the profile schema; NAICS lives in state.
